title: dodecatheon viviparum
intro: Dodecatheon viviparum, also known as the western sibbaldia, is a perennial wildflower native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are derived from its roots and leaves. The medicinal properties of Dodecatheon viviparum make it beneficial for treating various health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, as well as providing relief from pain and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture, or infused into oil for topical application.
